docs.title: Confluence MCP Server
docs.text: An MCP server for Confluence with safe handling of Confluence Storage Format (XHTML).

docs.title: The Problem
docs.text:
When AI assistants interact with Confluence via MCP servers, they often corrupt pages - especially tables - because:

  1. LLMs generate Markdown or HTML5 instead of Confluence Storage XHTML
  2. Tables require specific structure (<tbody>, no <thead>)
  3. Macros need ac: namespaces
  4. Round-tripping through incorrect formats causes data loss

docs.title: The Solution
docs.text:
This library provides:

  • Structured IR (Intermediate Representation): Work with Go types instead of raw XHTML
  • Safe Rendering: IR to valid Storage XHTML with proper structure
  • Validation: Catch forbidden tags, missing <tbody>, etc. before API calls
  • MCP Server: Tools that accept structured JSON, never raw XHTML

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• 8 MCP tools for reading, creating, updating, and searching Confluence pages
  • Structured content blocks - JSON instead of raw XHTML
  • Safe table handling - Proper <tbody> structure, no <thead>
  • Macro support - Status badges, info panels, code blocks
  • Vault-backed credentials - 1Password, Bitwarden, Keeper support

docs.title: Available Tools
docs.text: Tool Description confluence_read_page Read a page as structured blocks confluence_read_page_xhtml Read a page as raw Storage Format XHTML confluence_update_page Update a page with structured blocks confluence_update_page_xhtml Update a page with raw Storage Format XHTML confluence_create_page Create a new page with structured blocks confluence_create_table Create a table block from structured data confluence_delete_page Delete a page confluence_search_pages Search pages using CQL

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Install
go install github.com/plexusone/mcp-confluence/cmd/mcp-confluence@latest

# Configure credentials
export CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL="https://example.atlassian.net/wiki"
export CONFLUENCE_USERNAME="user@example.com"
export CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN="your-api-token"

# Run as MCP server
mcp-confluence

# Or use CLI mode
mcp-confluence read-page 12345

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Installation - Install the server
  • Setup - Configure your credentials
  • Quick Start - Start using the tools
  • Tools Reference - Detailed tool documentation
  • Storage Format - Understanding block types

docs.title: Claude Desktop Configuration
docs.text: Configure Claude Desktop to use the Confluence MCP Server.

docs.title: Configuration File Location
docs.text: OS Path macOS ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json Windows %APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json Linux ~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json

docs.title: With Direct Credentials
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "confluence": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-confluence",
      "env": {
        "CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL": "https://example.atlassian.net/wiki",
        "CONFLUENCE_USERNAME": "user@example.com",
        "CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN": "your-api-token"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: With 1Password
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "confluence": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-confluence",
      "env": {
        "CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL": "https://example.atlassian.net/wiki",
        "OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN": "ops_...",
        "OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI": "op://MyVault",
        "OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME": "confluence"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: With Bitwarden
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "confluence": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-confluence",
      "env": {
        "CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL": "https://example.atlassian.net/wiki",
        "BW_ACCESS_TOKEN": "...",
        "BW_ORGANIZATION_ID": "...",
        "OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI": "bw://org-id",
        "OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME": "confluence"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: With Keeper
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "confluence": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-confluence",
      "env": {
        "CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL": "https://example.atlassian.net/wiki",
        "KSM_TOKEN": "US:...",
        "OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI": "keeper://",
        "OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME": "confluence"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Environment Variables Reference
docs.text: Variable Description CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL Confluence instance URL CONFLUENCE_USERNAME Confluence username/email CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN Confluence API token OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI Vault URI (e.g., op://MyVault) OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME Credential name in vault (default: confluence) OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN 1Password service account token BW_ACCESS_TOKEN Bitwarden access token BW_ORGANIZATION_ID Bitwarden organization ID KSM_TOKEN Keeper token (format: REGION:TOKEN)

docs.title: Multiple Servers
docs.text:
You can run multiple MCP servers alongside Confluence:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "confluence": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-confluence",
      "env": {
        "CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL": "https://example.atlassian.net/wiki",
        "CONFLUENCE_USERNAME": "user@example.com",
        "CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN": "token"
      }
    },
    "google": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-google",
      "env": {
        "GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE": "/path/to/service-account.json"
      }
    },
    "aha": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-aha",
      "env": {
        "AHA_DOMAIN": "mycompany",
        "AHA_API_TOKEN": "token"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Server Not Starting
docs.text:
Check the Claude Desktop logs:

  • macOS: ~/Library/Logs/Claude/
  • Windows: %APPDATA%\Claude\logs\

Common issues:

  1. Binary not found: Verify the path is correct
  2. Credentials not found: Check environment variables
  3. Permission denied: Ensure the binary is executable (chmod +x)
  4. Invalid URL: Check CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL format

docs.title: Verifying Configuration
docs.text:
Test the server manually:

# Should start and wait for input (Ctrl+C to exit)
/path/to/mcp-confluence --base-url https://example.atlassian.net/wiki \
                        --username user@example.com \
                        --api-token your-token

docs.title: JSON Syntax Errors
docs.text:
Validate your JSON:

# On macOS/Linux
cat ~/Library/Application\ Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json | python3 -m json.tool

docs.title: Available Tools in Claude
docs.text:
Once configured, you can ask Claude to:

  • "Read Confluence page 12345"
  • "Search for pages about authentication"
  • "Create a new page in TEAM space"
  • "Update page 12345 with a status table"
  • "Delete page 67890"

docs.title: Credentials
docs.text: The Confluence MCP Server supports multiple credential sources for authentication.

docs.title: Option 1: Direct Credentials
docs.text: Provide your Confluence URL, username, and API token directly.

docs.title: Confluence Cloud
docs.text:
1. Go to Atlassian Account Settings
  2. Create a new API token
  3. Use your email as the username

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
mcp-confluence --base-url https://example.atlassian.net/wiki \
               --username user@example.com \
               --api-token your-api-token

Or with environment variables:

export CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL="https://example.atlassian.net/wiki"
export CONFLUENCE_USERNAME="user@example.com"
export CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN="your-api-token"
mcp-confluence

docs.title: Option 2: Vault-Backed Credentials
docs.text:
Use omnitoken with vault backends for secure credential storage.

Note: When using vault credentials, you still need to provide --base-url separately.

docs.title: Supported Vault URIs
docs.text: Provider URI Pattern Requirements 1Password op://vault OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN env var Bitwarden bw://org-id BW_ACCESS_TOKEN and BW_ORGANIZATION_ID env vars Keeper keeper:// KSM_TOKEN or KSM_CONFIG env var File file:///path/to/dir None

docs.title: 1Password
docs.text:
export OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN="ops_..."
mcp-confluence --vault op://MyVault --credentials-name confluence \
               --base-url https://example.atlassian.net/wiki

docs.title: Bitwarden
docs.text:
export BW_ACCESS_TOKEN="..."
export BW_ORGANIZATION_ID="..."
mcp-confluence --vault bw://org-id --credentials-name confluence \
               --base-url https://example.atlassian.net/wiki

docs.title: Keeper
docs.text:
export KSM_TOKEN="US:..."
mcp-confluence --vault keeper:// --credentials-name confluence \
               --base-url https://example.atlassian.net/wiki

docs.title: File Vault
docs.text:
For local development:

mcp-confluence --vault file:///path/to/secrets --credentials-name confluence \
               --base-url https://example.atlassian.net/wiki

docs.title: Credential Format
docs.text:
When using vault storage, credentials should be in goauth format:

{
  "type": "basic",
  "basic": {
    "username": "user@example.com",
    "password": "your-api-token",
    "serverURL": "https://example.atlassian.net/wiki"
  }
}

Or with OAuth2/Bearer token:

{
  "type": "headerquery",
  "headerQuery": {
    "serverURL": "https://example.atlassian.net/wiki",
    "header": {
      "Authorization": ["Bearer your-token"]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Security Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Never commit credentials - Add credentials files to .gitignore
  2. Use vault backends - For production, use proper secrets management
  3. Rotate tokens - Periodically rotate API tokens
  4. Limit scope - Use tokens with minimum required permissions

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: All command-line flags can be set via environment variables.

docs.title: Credential Configuration
docs.text: Variable Flag Description CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL --base-url Confluence instance URL CONFLUENCE_USERNAME --username Confluence username/email CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN --api-token Confluence API token OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI --vault Vault URI for credentials OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME --credentials-name Name of credentials in vault

docs.title: Vault Provider Authentication
docs.text: Variable Description OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN 1Password service account token BW_ACCESS_TOKEN Bitwarden access token BW_ORGANIZATION_ID Bitwarden organization ID KSM_TOKEN Keeper token (format: REGION:TOKEN) KSM_CONFIG Keeper config (base64-encoded JSON)

docs.title: Precedence
docs.text:
Command-line flags take precedence over environment variables.

# Environment variable is used
export CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL=https://example.atlassian.net/wiki
mcp-confluence
# Uses: https://example.atlassian.net/wiki

# Flag overrides environment
export CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL=https://example.atlassian.net/wiki
mcp-confluence --base-url https://other.atlassian.net/wiki
# Uses: https://other.atlassian.net/wiki

docs.title: Direct Credentials
docs.text:
export CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL="https://example.atlassian.net/wiki"
export CONFLUENCE_USERNAME="user@example.com"
export CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN="your-api-token"
mcp-confluence

docs.title: 1Password
docs.text:
export OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN="ops_..."
export OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI=op://MyVault
export OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME=confluence
export CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL="https://example.atlassian.net/wiki"
mcp-confluence

docs.title: Bitwarden
docs.text:
export BW_ACCESS_TOKEN="..."
export BW_ORGANIZATION_ID="..."
export OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI=bw://org-id
export OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME=confluence
export CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL="https://example.atlassian.net/wiki"
mcp-confluence

docs.title: Keeper
docs.text:
export KSM_TOKEN="US:..."
export OMNITOKEN_VAULT_URI=keeper://
export OMNITOKEN_CREDENTIALS_NAME=confluence
export CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL="https://example.atlassian.net/wiki"
mcp-confluence

docs.title: Bash/Zsh
docs.text:
Add to ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc:

# Confluence MCP Server credentials
export CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL="https://example.atlassian.net/wiki"
export CONFLUENCE_USERNAME="user@example.com"
export CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN="your-api-token"

docs.title: Fish
docs.text:
Add to ~/.config/fish/config.fish:

set -gx CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL "https://example.atlassian.net/wiki"
set -gx CONFLUENCE_USERNAME "user@example.com"
set -gx CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN "your-api-token"

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Go 1.24 or later
  • A Confluence Cloud or Data Center instance
  • Confluence API token (Cloud) or credentials (Data Center)

docs.title: Install from Source
docs.text: go install github.com/plexusone/mcp-confluence/cmd/mcp-confluence@latest

docs.title: Build from Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/plexusone/mcp-confluence.git
cd mcp-confluence
go build ./cmd/mcp-confluence

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text: mcp-confluence version

docs.title: As a Library
docs.text:
You can also use the packages directly:

go get github.com/plexusone/mcp-confluence

docs.title: Available Packages
docs.text: Package Description storage IR types, render, parse, validate for Confluence Storage Format confluence REST API client with IR integration mcpserver MCP server implementation

docs.title: 1. Configure Claude Desktop
docs.text:
Add to your Claude Desktop configuration file:

macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json Windows: %APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "confluence": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-confluence",
      "env": {
        "CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL": "https://example.atlassian.net/wiki",
        "CONFLUENCE_USERNAME": "user@example.com",
        "CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN": "your-api-token"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: 2. Restart Claude Desktop
docs.text: Close and reopen Claude Desktop to load the new configuration.

docs.title: 3. Start Using
docs.text:
You can now ask Claude to interact with Confluence:

  • "Read the Confluence page with ID 12345"
  • "Search for pages about authentication"
  • "Create a new page in TEAM space titled 'Meeting Notes'"
  • "Update page 12345 with a new table"

docs.title: Using the CLI
docs.text:
The server also works as a CLI tool for testing and scripting:

# Read a page as structured blocks
mcp-confluence read-page 12345

# Read a page as raw XHTML
mcp-confluence read-page-xhtml 12345

# Search for pages
mcp-confluence search-pages "type=page AND text~'authentication'" --limit 10

# Delete a page
mcp-confluence delete-page 12345

# Pretty print output
mcp-confluence read-page 12345 --output pretty

docs.title: Read and Understand a Page
docs.text:
You: Read the Confluence page 12345

Claude: I'll read that page for you.
[Uses confluence_read_page tool]

The page "API Design Guidelines" contains:
- Heading: REST API Standards
- Paragraph: Our API follows RESTful principles...
- Table with columns: Endpoint, Method, Description
...

docs.title: Create a New Page
docs.text:
You: Create a meeting notes page in the TEAM space

Claude: I'll create a new meeting notes page.
[Uses confluence_create_page tool with structured blocks]

Created page "Meeting Notes 2025-01-15" with ID 67890
URL: https://example.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/TEAM/pages/67890

docs.title: Update a Table
docs.text:
You: Add a new row to the status table on page 12345

Claude: I'll read the current page and add a new row.
[Uses confluence_read_page, then confluence_update_page]

Updated page with new table row:
| Service | Status |
|---------|--------|
| API     | OK     |
| Auth    | OK     |  <- New row added

docs.title: Why Structured Blocks?
docs.text:
This server uses structured JSON blocks instead of raw XHTML because:

  1. LLMs produce JSON reliably - No risk of malformed XHTML
  2. Tables are safe - Proper <tbody> structure is guaranteed
  3. Macros work correctly - Status badges, code blocks render properly
  4. Round-trip safe - Read, modify, write without corruption

docs.title: Confluence Cloud
docs.text:
1. Go to Atlassian Account Settings
  2. Create a new API token
  3. Note your email address (username)
  4. Note your Confluence URL (e.g., https://example.atlassian.net/wiki)

docs.title: Confluence Data Center
docs.text: Use your regular username and password, or configure an application link.

docs.title: Option 1: Environment Variables
docs.text:
export CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL="https://example.atlassian.net/wiki"
export CONFLUENCE_USERNAME="user@example.com"
export CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN="your-api-token"

docs.title: Option 2: Command-Line Flags
docs.text:
mcp-confluence --base-url https://example.atlassian.net/wiki \
               --username user@example.com \
               --api-token your-api-token

docs.title: Option 3: Vault-Backed Credentials
docs.text:
For production use, store credentials in a vault:

# 1Password
export OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN="ops_..."
mcp-confluence --vault op://MyVault --credentials-name confluence \
               --base-url https://example.atlassian.net/wiki

# Bitwarden
export BW_ACCESS_TOKEN="..."
export BW_ORGANIZATION_ID="..."
mcp-confluence --vault bw://org-id --credentials-name confluence \
               --base-url https://example.atlassian.net/wiki

# Keeper
export KSM_TOKEN="US:..."
mcp-confluence --vault keeper:// --credentials-name confluence \
               --base-url https://example.atlassian.net/wiki

See Credentials for detailed configuration options.

docs.title: Test Your Setup
docs.text:
# Test with CLI mode - search for pages
mcp-confluence search-pages "type=page" --limit 5

docs.title: Release Notes - v0.1.0
docs.text: Release Date: December 29, 2025

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Initial release of mcp-confluence, an MCP server for Confluence with safe handling of Confluence Storage Format (XHTML).

This release addresses a critical problem: AI assistants corrupting Confluence pages when editing them, especially tables and macros.

docs.title: MCP Tools
docs.text: Tool Description confluence_read_page Read a page as structured content blocks confluence_read_page_xhtml Read a page as raw Storage Format XHTML confluence_update_page Update a page with structured content blocks confluence_update_page_xhtml Update a page with raw Storage Format XHTML confluence_create_page Create a new page with structured content blocks confluence_create_table Create a table block from structured data confluence_delete_page Delete a page by ID confluence_search_pages Search pages using CQL (Confluence Query Language)

docs.title: Structured Blocks
docs.text:
The following block types are supported for creating and reading pages:

  • Paragraph - Text paragraphs
  • Heading - H1-H6 headings
  • Table - Tables with headers, rows, and optional macros in cells
  • BulletList - Unordered lists
  • NumberedList - Ordered lists
  • Macro - Confluence macros (status, info, code, etc.)
  • CodeBlock - Code blocks with language
  • HorizontalRule - Horizontal dividers

docs.title: Raw XHTML Support
docs.text:
For editing existing pages with complex content, the XHTML tools provide:

  • Lossless round-trip editing - No data loss on read/modify/write cycles
  • Full attribute preservation - Column widths, styles, IDs maintained
  • Nested content support - Lists, bold, links inside table cells
  • Macro preservation - ac: namespace macros kept intact
  • Validation - XHTML validated before sending to Confluence API

docs.title: Storage Package
docs.text:
The storage package provides:

  • Parse - Convert Storage Format XHTML to structured blocks
  • Render - Convert structured blocks to valid Storage Format XHTML
  • Validate - Check XHTML for forbidden tags, missing <tbody>, etc.
  • HTML Entity Support - Full support for HTML entities (•, →, etc.)

docs.title: Confluence Client
docs.text:
The confluence package provides a REST API client with:

  • Basic authentication support
  • Page CRUD operations
  • CQL search support
  • Both structured (IR) and raw XHTML methods

docs.title: Recommended Usage
docs.text:
Scenario Recommended Tool Create new page confluence_create_page (blocks) Read simple page confluence_read_page (blocks) Read complex page confluence_read_page_xhtml Edit existing page confluence_update_page_xhtml Edit tables Always confluence_update_page_xhtml

Key guidance: - Creating pages? Use blocks - simpler, guaranteed valid output - Editing pages? Use XHTML - preserves everything, no data loss

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go install github.com/plexusone/mcp-confluence/cmd/mcp-confluence@latest

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL Your Confluence instance URL CONFLUENCE_USERNAME Your Confluence username (usually email) CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN API token from Atlassian Account Settings

docs.title: Claude Code Integration
docs.text:
Add to ~/.claude.json or .mcp.json:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "confluence": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-confluence",
      "env": {
        "CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL": "https://example.atlassian.net/wiki",
        "CONFLUENCE_USERNAME": "user@example.com",
        "CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN": "your-api-token"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• OAuth authentication not yet supported (API token only)
  • Attachment handling not yet implemented
  • Page labels/metadata not yet supported
  • Confluence Data Center/Server not tested (Cloud only)

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• Repository: https://github.com/plexusone/mcp-confluence
  • Documentation: See README.md
  • Roadmap: See ROADMAP.md

docs.title: Release Notes v0.2.0
docs.text: Major architectural upgrade to a composable omniskill-based MCP server with vault-backed credentials.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Composable Architecture: Built on omniskill for modular, reusable skills
  • Vault-Backed Credentials: Secure credential storage via 1Password, Bitwarden, and more
  • Cobra CLI: New command-line interface with subcommands and direct tool invocation
  • Module Rename: Moved from agentplexus to plexusone organization

docs.title: Breaking Changes
docs.text:
• Module path changed from github.com/agentplexus/mcp-confluence to github.com/plexusone/mcp-confluence
  • CLI now uses cobra with --flag style instead of -flag

docs.title: Composable Skill Architecture
docs.text:
The Confluence skill can now be imported into any omniskill-based MCP server:

import (
    confskill "github.com/plexusone/mcp-confluence/skills/confluence"
    runtime "github.com/plexusone/omniskill/mcp/server"
)

// Add to your omniskill runtime
skill := confskill.New(httpClient, baseURL)
skill.Init(ctx)
rt.RegisterSkill(skill)

docs.title: Vault-Backed Credentials
docs.text:
Three authentication methods now supported:

Method Description Direct Credentials Base URL, username, API token via flags/env 1Password Via OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN Bitwarden Via BW_ACCESS_TOKEN and BW_ORGANIZATION_ID

docs.title: Cobra CLI
docs.text:
New subcommand structure:

mcp-confluence                    # Run MCP server (default)
mcp-confluence get-page <id>      # Direct tool invocation
mcp-confluence search-pages <cql> # Search pages
mcp-confluence --help             # Show all commands

docs.title: API Testing Example
docs.text: New cmd/api-confluence for testing the Confluence client API directly.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
Comprehensive MkDocs documentation site added:

  • Getting Started (installation, setup, quickstart)
  • Configuration (credentials, environment, Claude Desktop)
  • Storage Format (overview, blocks reference)
  • Tools Reference

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: Dependency Version Purpose github.com/plexusone/omniskill v0.8.0 Skill framework github.com/plexusone/omnitoken v0.1.0 Token/credential management github.com/plexusone/omnivault-desktop v0.1.0 Vault provider registration github.com/spf13/cobra v1.10.2 CLI framework

docs.title: Update Installation
docs.text:
# Old
go install github.com/agentplexus/mcp-confluence/cmd/mcp-confluence@latest

# New
go install github.com/plexusone/mcp-confluence/cmd/mcp-confluence@latest

docs.title: Update Imports
docs.text:
// Old
import "github.com/agentplexus/mcp-confluence/confluence"

// New
import "github.com/plexusone/mcp-confluence/confluence"

docs.title: Update Claude Desktop Configuration
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "confluence": {
      "command": "/path/to/mcp-confluence",
      "env": {
        "CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL": "https://example.atlassian.net/wiki",
        "CONFLUENCE_USERNAME": "user@example.com",
        "CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN": "your-token"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Block Types
docs.text: This page documents all supported block types for structured content.

docs.title: Paragraph
docs.text:
Basic text content.

JSON:

{"type": "paragraph", "text": "This is a paragraph."}

Renders to:

<p>This is a paragraph.</p>

docs.title: Heading
docs.text:
H1-H6 headings.

JSON:

{"type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "Section Title"}

Renders to:

<h2>Section Title</h2>

docs.title: Table
docs.text:
Tables with headers and rows.

JSON:

{
  "type": "table",
  "headers": ["Name", "Role", "Status"],
  "rows": [
    ["Alice", "Lead", "Active"],
    ["Bob", "Developer", "Active"]
  ]
}

Renders to:

<table>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <th>Name</th>
      <th>Role</th>
      <th>Status</th>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Alice</td>
      <td>Lead</td>
      <td>Active</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>Bob</td>
      <td>Developer</td>
      <td>Active</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

Note: Headers are rendered as <th> in the first row of <tbody>. Confluence Storage Format does NOT support <thead>.

docs.title: Tables with Macros
docs.text:
Cells can contain macros:

{
  "type": "table",
  "headers": ["Service", "Status"],
  "rows": [
    ["API", {"macro": {"name": "status", "params": {"colour": "Green", "title": "OK"}}}]
  ]
}

docs.title: Bullet List
docs.text:
Unordered lists.

JSON:

{"type": "bullet_list", "items": ["First item", "Second item", "Third item"]}

Renders to:

<ul>
  <li>First item</li>
  <li>Second item</li>
  <li>Third item</li>
</ul>

docs.title: Numbered List
docs.text:
Ordered lists.

JSON:

{"type": "numbered_list", "items": ["Step one", "Step two", "Step three"]}

Renders to:

<ol>
  <li>Step one</li>
  <li>Step two</li>
  <li>Step three</li>
</ol>

docs.title: Macro
docs.text: Confluence macros.

docs.title: Status Macro
docs.text:
JSON:

{
  "type": "macro",
  "name": "status",
  "params": {
    "colour": "Green",
    "title": "OK"
  }
}

Renders to:

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="status">
  <ac:parameter ac:name="colour">Green</ac:parameter>
  <ac:parameter ac:name="title">OK</ac:parameter>
</ac:structured-macro>

Available colors: Grey, Red, Yellow, Green, Blue

docs.title: Info Macro
docs.text:
JSON:

{
  "type": "macro",
  "name": "info",
  "params": {"title": "Note"},
  "body": "This is important information."
}

docs.title: Warning Macro
docs.text:
JSON:

{
  "type": "macro",
  "name": "warning",
  "params": {"title": "Warning"},
  "body": "Be careful!"
}

docs.title: Code Macro
docs.text:
JSON:

{
  "type": "macro",
  "name": "code",
  "params": {"language": "python"},
  "body": "print('Hello, World!')"
}

docs.title: Code Block
docs.text:
Shorthand for code macro.

JSON:

{"type": "code_block", "language": "go", "code": "fmt.Println(\"Hello\")"}

Renders to:

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="code">
  <ac:parameter ac:name="language">go</ac:parameter>
  <ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[fmt.Println("Hello")]]></ac:plain-text-body>
</ac:structured-macro>

docs.title: Horizontal Rule
docs.text:
Divider line.

JSON:

{"type": "horizontal_rule"}

Renders to:

<hr/>

docs.title: Storage Format Overview
docs.text: Confluence uses a specific XHTML dialect called "Storage Format" for page content. This server uses structured blocks as an intermediate representation (IR) to safely work with this format.

docs.title: The Problem
docs.text:
When AI assistants work directly with Confluence XHTML:

  1. Wrong format - LLMs generate HTML5 or Markdown, not Storage Format
  2. Table corruption - Missing <tbody>, incorrect <thead> usage
  3. Broken macros - Missing ac: namespaces
  4. Data loss - Round-tripping through incorrect formats

docs.title: The Solution
docs.text:
Structured blocks solve these problems:

  1. JSON format - LLMs produce JSON reliably
  2. Safe rendering - Go code generates valid XHTML
  3. Validation - Errors caught before API calls
  4. Reversible - Parse XHTML back to blocks

docs.title: Block Types
docs.text:
Type Description Paragraph Text paragraph Heading H1-H6 headings Table Tables with headers and rows BulletList Unordered lists NumberedList Ordered lists Macro Confluence macros (status, info, code) CodeBlock Code blocks with language HorizontalRule Horizontal dividers

See Block Types for detailed documentation.

docs.title: 1. AI receives task
docs.text: "Add a status table to page 12345"

docs.title: 2. AI produces JSON
docs.text:
{
  "blocks": [
    {"type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "Service Status"},
    {
      "type": "table",
      "headers": ["Service", "Status"],
      "rows": [
        ["API", {"macro": {"name": "status", "params": {"colour": "Green", "title": "OK"}}}],
        ["Auth", {"macro": {"name": "status", "params": {"colour": "Green", "title": "OK"}}}]
      ]
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: 3. Server renders XHTML
docs.text:
<h2>Service Status</h2>
<table>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <th>Service</th>
      <th>Status</th>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>API</td>
      <td><ac:structured-macro ac:name="status">
        <ac:parameter ac:name="colour">Green</ac:parameter>
        <ac:parameter ac:name="title">OK</ac:parameter>
      </ac:structured-macro></td>
    </tr>
    ...
  </tbody>
</table>

docs.title: 4. Server validates
docs.text:
• Checks for forbidden tags (<thead>, <script>)
  • Verifies <tbody> structure
  • Validates macro namespaces

docs.title: 5. Updates Confluence
docs.text: Valid XHTML is sent to the Confluence API.

docs.title: Tools Reference
docs.text: This server provides 8 tools for working with Confluence pages.

docs.title: confluence_read_page
docs.text:
Read a page as structured blocks.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description page_id string Yes The page ID

Example:

{
  "name": "confluence_read_page",
  "arguments": {
    "page_id": "12345"
  }
}

Response:

{
  "page_id": "12345",
  "title": "API Design Guidelines",
  "version": 5,
  "blocks": [
    {"type": "heading", "level": 1, "text": "Overview"},
    {"type": "paragraph", "text": "This document describes..."},
    {"type": "table", "headers": ["Method", "Path"], "rows": [...]}
  ]
}

docs.title: confluence_read_page_xhtml
docs.text:
Read a page as raw Storage Format XHTML.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description page_id string Yes The page ID

Response:

{
  "page_id": "12345",
  "title": "API Design Guidelines",
  "version": 5,
  "xhtml": "<h1>Overview</h1><p>This document describes...</p>"
}

Use this when you need to preserve complex formatting or debug parsing issues.

docs.title: confluence_create_page
docs.text:
Create a new page with structured blocks.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description space_key string Yes Space key (e.g., "TEAM") title string Yes Page title parent_id string No Parent page ID blocks array Yes Content blocks

Example:

{
  "name": "confluence_create_page",
  "arguments": {
    "space_key": "TEAM",
    "title": "Meeting Notes 2025-01-15",
    "parent_id": "11111",
    "blocks": [
      {"type": "heading", "level": 1, "text": "Meeting Notes"},
      {"type": "paragraph", "text": "Attendees: Alice, Bob, Carol"},
      {"type": "bullet_list", "items": ["Review PR #123", "Update documentation"]}
    ]
  }
}

docs.title: confluence_update_page
docs.text:
Update a page with structured blocks.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description page_id string Yes The page ID title string No New title (optional) blocks array Yes Content blocks

Example:

{
  "name": "confluence_update_page",
  "arguments": {
    "page_id": "12345",
    "title": "Updated Page Title",
    "blocks": [
      {"type": "heading", "level": 1, "text": "Updated Content"},
      {"type": "paragraph", "text": "This page has been updated."}
    ]
  }
}

docs.title: confluence_update_page_xhtml
docs.text:
Update a page with raw Storage Format XHTML.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description page_id string Yes The page ID title string No New title (optional) xhtml string Yes Raw XHTML content

Use this when you need to preserve complex formatting.

docs.title: confluence_create_table
docs.text:
Create a table block from structured data.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description headers array Yes Column headers rows array Yes Row data (2D array)

Example:

{
  "name": "confluence_create_table",
  "arguments": {
    "headers": ["Service", "Owner", "Status"],
    "rows": [
      ["Auth", "Platform", {"macro": {"name": "status", "params": {"colour": "Green", "title": "OK"}}}],
      ["API", "Backend", {"macro": {"name": "status", "params": {"colour": "Yellow", "title": "Degraded"}}}]
    ]
  }
}

docs.title: confluence_delete_page
docs.text:
Delete a page.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description page_id string Yes The page ID

docs.title: confluence_search_pages
docs.text:
Search pages using CQL (Confluence Query Language).

Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description cql string Yes CQL query limit integer No Maximum results

Example:

{
  "name": "confluence_search_pages",
  "arguments": {
    "cql": "space=TEAM and type=page and title~\"Meeting\"",
    "limit": 10
  }
}
